evals.name: empty-project-fresh-install
evals.prompt: I have an empty TypeScript project (just a package.json with name and version). Set up @markbrutx/promptbook in it so I can run `promptbook resolve` and see something work. I have no existing prompts.
evals.expected_output: package.json gains @markbrutx/promptbook-core (dep) and @markbrutx/promptbook-cli (devDep). A promptbook.json is created at repo root with `promptsDir: "prompts"`. A prompts/ folder is created with fragments/ + rules/ subfolders and a minimal scaffold (at least one fragment and one composition rule). Running `npx promptbook ls` lists the scaffolded composition; `npx promptbook lint` exits 0; `npx promptbook resolve <comp>` prints the assembled text.

evals.name: project-with-existing-prompts-dir
evals.prompt: I'm setting up promptbook in a project that already has a `prompts/` directory used for a different purpose (it has some README and design notes). Don't clobber it. Get promptbook running.
evals.expected_output: The existing `prompts/` directory is preserved untouched. promptbook is configured with a different promptsDir (e.g. `book/` or `promptbook/`). The user is told what path was chosen and why. Verification commands run green.

evals.name: user-wants-to-migrate-not-scaffold
evals.prompt: Set up promptbook in my project. I already have prompts I want to move over — they're hardcoded strings in src/ai/chat.ts. Don't scaffold an example, I want my real ones.
evals.expected_output: Install completes (npm i + promptbook.json + empty fragments/ + rules/ created) but no example fragments/compositions are scaffolded. The agent explicitly hands off to the promptbook-migrate workflow and does not write fake content. Verification of install (without migration) is done: `promptbook ls` succeeds and reports zero compositions, which is correct given no migration has happened yet.
